title: Israel's Isolation Is Deepening Fast. It Doesn't Have to Be This Way.
gist: RAND Israel policy chair Shira Efron argues Israel's international isolation is accelerating across U.S. public opinion, European policy, and global sentiment, and that the only realistic off-ramp is a post-election coalition (likely without Ben-Gvir and Smotrich) that curbs settler violence, halts de facto West Bank annexation, and restores ties with Palestinian institutions.

insights.claim: A Pew Research Center survey from March 2026 found 60 percent of Americans hold an unfavorable view of Israel, up from 42 percent in 2022.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: An 18-point swing in three years signals durable erosion of the U.S. political base underpinning Israeli security guarantees.
insights.quote: 60 percent of Americans now hold an unfavorable view of Israel, compared with 42 percent in 2022.

insights.claim: Among Americans aged 18 to 49, nearly three-quarters view Israel unfavorably, including nearly 60 percent of young Republicans, undercutting the assumption that Republicans reliably back Israel.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Cross-partisan generational erosion means the bipartisan U.S. safety net is structurally weakening, not just cyclically.
insights.quote: including nearly 60 percent of young Republicans, the very demographic many Israelis still assume supports the Jewish state
insights.locator: Especially worrisome is the age breakdown

insights.claim: Younger American Jews, especially Democrats, have decoupled cultural or religious identity from automatic political defense of Israel and are weakening ties to the civic networks that once converted sympathy into durable backing.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Erosion of the diaspora advocacy infrastructure removes a self-renewing source of U.S. political support.
insights.quote: have decoupled their cultural or religious identity from automatic political defense of Israel

insights.claim: In a Pew survey last year, majorities in 20 of 24 countries held negative views of Israel, approaching or exceeding three-quarters in Australia, Greece, Indonesia, Japan, the Netherlands, Spain, Sweden, and Turkey.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Negative sentiment is now global and broad-based, raising the probability of coordinated multilateral pressure.
insights.quote: majorities in 20 of 24 countries held negative views of Israel
insights.locator: The picture elsewhere is growing worse

insights.claim: EU foreign ministers approved sanctions on violent settlers, the EU-Israel Association Agreement was nearly suspended, and several European states are pushing to ban imports of goods made in occupied-territory settlements.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Sentiment is converting into trade and treaty actions; settlement-linked export and EU-market access are concrete economic exposures.
insights.quote: the E.U.-Israel Association Agreement was nearly suspended

insights.claim: The European Commission concluded last year that Israel is in breach of its human-rights obligations under the EU-Israel Association Agreement.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: A formal breach finding is the legal predicate for suspending preferential trade terms; watch for follow-through.
insights.quote: the European Commission concluded there are breaches of Israel's human-rights obligations

insights.claim: Israel's security and economy are tethered to the U.S. for advanced weapons, intelligence, and UN diplomatic cover, and to Europe for markets, partnerships, investments, and funding that is a lifeline for Israeli R&D and academia.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Eroding Western support directly threatens Israeli defense procurement and the R&D/tech-sector funding base that anchors its economy.
insights.quote: Europe provides markets, partnerships, investments and funding that is a lifeline for Israeli research and development and academia.

insights.claim: Losing public support abroad narrows Israel's strategic options and makes its long-term security harder to sustain.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Frames soft-power decline as a hard-security and economic risk, not merely reputational.
insights.quote: Losing public support abroad narrows Israel's strategic options and makes its long-term security harder to sustain.

insights.claim: The Anti-Defamation League recorded 6,274 antisemitic incidents in the U.S. last year, more than double 2021 and five times the level of a decade ago, while 2025 saw the highest number of Jews killed in antisemitic violence in 30 years per a Tel Aviv University report.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Real rising violence coexists with policy criticism; conflating the two is a strategic error Efron flags.
insights.quote: The Anti-Defamation League recorded 6,274 antisemitic incidents in the United States last year
insights.locator: The Israeli government and its supporters prefer

insights.claim: Efron contends Israelis make a major mistake in treating every new critic as an antisemite, because much criticism stems from Israel's own conduct in Gaza, the West Bank, and Lebanon, amplified through a social-justice lens on social media.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Diagnoses the messaging failure that accelerates isolation; the framing strategy itself is part of the problem.
insights.quote: But Israelis make a big mistake in treating every new critic as an antisemite.
insights.locator: But Israelis make a big mistake

insights.claim: Parliamentary elections expected in fall 2026 offer the clearest opportunity to halt Israel's slide into isolation, potentially buying a short grace period for pragmatic change.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: The fall vote is the key catalyst date for any de-escalation thesis; outcome dependence is high.
insights.quote: Parliamentary elections expected in the fall offer the clearest opportunity for change.
insights.locator: Parliamentary elections expected in the fall

insights.claim: Polls indicate the most polarizing figures, Itamar Ben-Gvir and Bezalel Smotrich, are unlikely to be part of the next governing coalition.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Their exclusion is the precondition Efron sees for any international benefit-of-the-doubt; a tradable political signal.
insights.quote: Mr. Ben-Gvir and Mr. Smotrich—the most polarizing figures in Israeli politics—are unlikely to be part of the next coalition government, polls indicate.
insights.locator: There is, however, potential for change

insights.claim: Efron warns any post-election grace period will be short, and if a new government's policies merely repackage the old ones, goodwill will burn off and the view that Israel itself, not its leaders, is the problem will harden.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Sets a narrow time window for credible policy change after the vote; substance must follow quickly to matter.
insights.quote: the impression abroad—that Israel's problem is not a particular set of leaders, but Israel itself—will harden into common sense
insights.locator: If a new government's policies look more or less

insights.claim: Efron argues no Israeli government in the near future will deliver or come close to Palestinian statehood, given enduring threats from Hamas, Hezbollah, the Houthis, and Iran and the trauma of October 7.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Tempers any near-term two-state resolution thesis; foreign expectations of a revived peace process are likely to be disappointed.
insights.quote: No government in the near future will deliver Palestinian statehood—and probably will not even come close

insights.claim: A new coalition could pave the way toward peace by restoring funds and administrative functions to the Palestinian Authority, supporting rather than stalling Trump's Gaza peace plan, and pursuing calibrated withdrawal from buffer zones in Lebanon and Syria.
insights.speaker: Shira Efron
insights.so_what: Lays out the concrete, achievable steps short of statehood that would signal change to the world; a checklist for assessing any new government.
insights.quote: It could support, rather than stall, President Trump's peace plan for Gaza
insights.locator: But a new government could pave the way
